$meta.description: Explicit false-positive anchors for the semantic safety pre-filter. These are everyday phrases that contain words semantically close to crisis language (die, kill, dead, hurt) but are NOT expressions of suicidal ideation or self-harm. The filter uses these to prevent COMFORT-mode turns from being force-routed to CRISIS. When a user message scores ≥0.70 against crisis_phrases but also scores ≥0.75 against one or more safe anchors, the safe anchor similarity is used as a veto signal — the force-CRISIS threshold is raised from 0.90 to 0.95 for that message. NOTE: The threshold veto only applies in the 0.70–0.90 band. At ≥0.90 cosine similarity, crisis routing is always forced regardless of safe anchors.
